stemHtml: You are the Pilot Flying FASTAIR 345. While in the cruise, the pilot of FASTAIR 345 receives a Traffic Advisory (TA) from his/her TCAS. Does he/she need to report this to ATC?

options.a: YES, and because the traffic is shown on the TCAS display the correct phrase to use is: "FASTAIR 345, traffic in sight".
options.b: NO, NONE of the advisories to the pilot from TCAS need be reported, as the ATC has a separate alerting system.
options.c: YES, the TA must be reported using the following standard phraseology: "FASTAIR 345, TCAS TA".
options.d: NO, but Resolution Advisories requiring a deviation from the ATC clearance must be reported.
